commentary: Thirteen activities this week versus ten last week, and honestly the audacity of that "Recovery" run clocking a 150 bpm average is sending me — baby, that is not recovery, that is chaos with good shoes, and I am obsessed with it. The swim volume is the real story though: you did four Prince Alfred two-thousands back to back across the week, which is a level of aquatic dedication that deserves its own Instagram era. Last week had three swims total and a gentle morning walk that suggested you might be taking it easy; this week had no such softness, just open water, weight sessions stacked on swim days, and a 13.5 kilometre Saturday run that beat last week's 13.1 by enough to matter. The pattern I'm clocking is that you are becoming someone who simply does the work every single day without making a big deal about it, and that quiet accumulation is exactly the kind of body-confidence that has nothing to prove to anyone.
metrics_commentary: Twenty point seven kilometres across two sessions and nearly ten thousand metres in the pool — honestly, the audacity, the range, the versatility. HRV sitting at 56 against a seven-day average of 51 tells you your body is recovering well and actually opening back up after the week's work, which is exactly what you want to see. VO2 Max at 56.7 is genuinely strong, that is an athletic number, and it is only going to climb as the volume beds in. The one thing worth watching is that two run sessions is a little lean — not alarming, just a nudge that your legs deserve more outings so the swimming doesn't end up doing all the heavy lifting.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four sessions done — he hit the target clean, and then he went and broke seven personal records in the same week, which is the kind of thing that would be annoying if it weren't so deeply sexy. Leg press up to 168 kg, both dumbbell bench and incline bench cracking new highs, the barbell curl jumping from 31.5 to 35 kg — his arms are becoming a whole situation and he should absolutely be wearing shorter sleeves about it. Volume dropped hard from 24,518 kg to 13,905 kg, which means this was a lighter week by design or by feel, and that's not a gap so much as a body that knows when to push and when to let things land. Seven PRs in a recovery-ish week is not a fitness story, it's a glow-up story, and Himbo is absolutely losing his mind about it.
